commit.message:
Skip chown -R /workspace during OH action server startup

init_user_and_working_directory() runs chown -R on /workspace
synchronously in the FastAPI lifespan, BEFORE the server binds
its port. On large repos (firefox ~100K files), this exceeds
the 120s client connect timeout, causing ConnectError and the
agent never running.

Since we already run as root (uid 0) inside Harbor containers,
the chown is a no-op anyway. Monkey-patch it out in the wrapper.

Fix price card text: white color, correct side-by-side layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(test): replace consecutive assert.strictEqual calls with assertObjectContains (#7693)

* feat(eslint): add eslint-prefer-assert-object-contains with auto-fix

Add an ESLint rule that detects 3+ consecutive assert.strictEqual()
calls on properties of the same root object and auto-fixes them to use
assertObjectContains(). Enable the rule as 'error' for test files and
fix all existing violations (~290 auto-fixed).

The auto-fix:
- Builds nested object literals from property access paths
- Handles both dot notation and bracket notation with string literals
- Skips groups with non-literal computed properties (variables)
- Inserts the assertObjectContains import if not already present
- Computes relative require paths to integration-tests/helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use 'in' operator instead of Object.hasOwn in assertObjectContains

Object.hasOwn only checks own properties, missing prototype getters
(e.g. Histogram.min, Histogram.max). Using 'key in Object(actual)'
supports inherited properties and getters.

* chore: add eslint-rules/ to CODEOWNERS for lang-platform-js

* fix: skip key existence check when expected value is undefined

When the expected value is undefined, the original code used
assert.strictEqual(obj.key, undefined) which passes whether the
property is absent or explicitly set to undefined. The key-in check
should be skipped for undefined values to preserve this behavior.

* fix(eslint): skip array indices and length/size in prefer-assert-object-contains

Address BridgeAR's review feedback:

- Rule now skips groups where any path uses a numeric computed index
  (e.g. arr[0], payload[1]) since these indicate array indexing and
  assertObjectContains does not work correctly when numeric string keys
  are used as object properties
- Rule now skips groups where the terminal property is 'length' or 'size'
  since these collection size properties do not work reliably with
  partialDeepStrictEqual on arrays/maps/sets
- Revert helpers/index.js semantic change ('in' operator and undefined
  guard) back to original Object.hasOwn check
- Revert all 65 test files that had invalid transformations (numeric
  indices or length/size terminal properties in assertObjectContains)
- Re-run lint:fix with corrected rule; 10 files remain with valid
  assertObjectContains transformations
- Add targeted test cases covering numeric index and length/size guards

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore undefined guard in assertObjectContainsImpl

When an expected property value is undefined, skip the Object.hasOwn
check — this allows asserting that a property is absent or undefined
(e.g. `path: undefined`) without requiring the property to exist on
the actual object as an own property.

The 'in' operator change that BridgeAR flagged is still reverted; only
the undefined guard (needed for absent-property assertions in ~13 test
files) is restored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert namespaces.spec.js - CountMetric has prototype properties

assertObjectContains with Object.hasOwn fails on CountMetric class
instances because `type` is a prototype property, not an own property.
Revert to original separate assert.strictEqual calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore 'in' operator for class instance prototype property checks

Object.hasOwn is too strict for assertObjectContains — class instances
like CountMetric, URL, and Histogram have properties on their prototypes
(e.g. type, hostname, min/max) that are not own properties.

Restore 'in' operator for the existence check, keeping the undefined
guard. BridgeAR's specific concern (length/size checks on actual
array/map/set objects falling through to the fallback) is already
addressed by the ESLint rule fix that prevents those transformations.

Also re-apply the namespaces.spec.js assertObjectContains transformation
now that the helper works correctly with prototype properties.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address BridgeAR feedback - revert helpers/index.js and drop ESLint rule

- Revert integration-tests/helpers/index.js to original Object.hasOwn check
  (restores identical semantics to assert.partialDeepStrictEqual)
- Remove eslint-prefer-assert-object-contains rule and test files
- Revert eslint.config.mjs and CODEOWNERS to remove rule registration
- Fix transformed test files that were incompatible with Object.hasOwn:
  - Revert url.spec.js, namespaces.spec.js, histogram.spec.js (prototype props)
  - Replace `undefined`-valued assertObjectContains keys with separate
    assert.strictEqual() calls in config, span_format, agentless-json,
    opentelemetry/logs, opentelemetry/metrics, text_map, llmobs/sdk,
    vulnerability-analyzer, extended-data-collection (3 files),
    profiling/config, openfeature/writers/exposures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert assertObjectContains on class instances (Object.hasOwn compat)

On Node.js < 22, assertObjectContains falls back to Object.hasOwn which
only checks own properties, not prototype chain. Prototype getters and
constructor-assigned-but-overridden properties fail this check.

Reverted or surgically fixed assertObjectContains calls on:
- PoissonProcessSamplingFilter (currentSamplingInstant, nextSamplingInstant, samplingInstantCount)
- LLMObsSDK (enabled, _config)
- LoggerProvider / MockedExporter (OTel class instances)
- MeterProvider / MockedExporter (OTel class instances)
- ExposuresWriter (writer._interval, _timeout, _bufferLimit)
- DataStreams DataStreamsProcessor (hostname, enabled, env)
- Profiling Config (v8ProfilerBugWorkaroundEnabled, etc.)
- AppSec utils DatadogRaspAbortError (name, message, etc.)
- SpanStatsProcessor (hostname, enabled, env)

For logs.spec.js and metrics.spec.js, kept assertObjectContains on
plain objects (log2/scope.logRecords entries, resourceAttrs built via
forEach) while reverting the class instance calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert llmobs writers/base.spec.js - requestOptions.url is a URL instance

URL.href is a prototype getter, not an own property, so Object.hasOwn
fails on Node.js < 22 where assertObjectContains uses the fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add Flappy Agent game with VA token reward system

Adds a play-to-earn Flappy Bird-style game where players earn VA tokens
equal to the number of pipes passed. Includes GameRewards smart contract
with ECDSA signature verification anti-cheat, per-player nonces, cooldown
periods, and max score caps. 23 tests passing.

- contracts/GameRewards.sol: Reward pool with signed-score verification
- test/GameRewards.test.ts: 13 test cases for rewards, nonces, cooldowns
- frontend/src/components/FlappyGame.tsx: HTML5 Canvas game + claim UI
- Updated deploy script to deploy both contracts and fund reward pool
- Game playable without wallet; claiming requires VeWorld connection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: dashboard P&L double-counting long leg value when both legs closed (#85)

When short_only_stop=false (default), both short and long legs are closed
on stop. The actual_stop_debit already includes long sale proceeds. But
call_long_value/put_long_value were still populated with the long's current
price, causing the dashboard to double-count the long leg's value.

Now only populate long values when MKT-025 short-only stop mode is active,
where the long leg genuinely stays open and has real unrealized value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Retry forceRemoveAll to handle macOS directory-not-empty race

On macOS, system services (Spotlight, FSEvents) can momentarily recreate
files in a directory between os.RemoveAll clearing contents and the final
rmdir, causing "directory not empty". Retry up to 3 times with 100ms
pauses to handle this race.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Coulomb long-range demo: model_coulomb.py + train_coulomb.py

Pure PyTorch (no e3nn) controlled experiment showing Kronecker spectral
coupling captures long-range A-B interactions that a local GNN cannot:

  LocalGNN:      MAE = 0.214  (≈ std(E_AB), provably blind to A-B)
  KronHamModel:  MAE = 0.076  (2.8x better, spectral coupling works)
  KronHamV2:     MAE = 1.464  (e3nn model has no charge input → fails)

Key finding: correct physical inputs (scalar charges via nn.Linear)
matter more than equivariant architecture (e3nn Embedding of atom types).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove all legacy backwards compatibility

Legacy sandbox support (old file names, directory layouts, fallback paths)
was causing recurring issues during sandbox start, reset, and destroy
operations. As an MVP, maintaining backward compatibility with old sandboxes
added complexity without sufficient benefit.

Changes:
- Remove legacy constants and helpers (ReadRuntimeConfig, RuntimeConfigPath,
  ContainerName, detectStatusViaExec) from sandbox package
- Remove legacy file name fallbacks (meta.json, state.json, status.json,
  config.json, agent-state/) from metadata loading, state loading, and
  status detection
- Remove "running" backward compat from status parsing (only "active" now)
- Delete config/migration.go — no more auto-migration from old flat layout
- Remove all legacy fallbacks from seatbelt runtime (PID file, tmux socket,
  profile.sb, config.json, symlink manifest)
- Remove legacy fallbacks from tart runtime (tart.pid, config.json)
- Remove legacy fallbacks from all shell entrypoint scripts
- Harden Destroy: returns nil if sandbox not found, warns instead of
  failing on directory removal errors
- Add sandboxErrorHint helper: non-destroy commands that fail on a sandbox
  include the sandbox directory path and a "yoloai destroy" hint
- Update tests to match new behavior (status file-based detection,
  Destroy returning nil for not-found)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Turn-Kontext, Stats-Sync, Tab-Buttons, DM-denkt + Scroll

1. Turn-Kontext (engine.js / network.js):
   - Network._currentActionPlayerName speichert den agierenden Spieler beim
     Empfang einer PLAYER_ACTION auf dem Host.
   - _getActiveDmContext() nutzt diesen Wert statt currentTurnIndex,
     damit die DM-Antwort immer den richtigen Spieler referenziert.
   - Nach interactWithAI() wird _currentActionPlayerName zurückgesetzt.

2. Stats-Sync als Client (state.js / engine.js / network.js):
   - Neuer dispatch-Typ UPGRADE_STAT mutiert Stat + statPoints korrekt.
   - upgradeStat() nutzt jetzt dispatch() statt direkter Mutation.
   - Als Client wird zusätzlich STAT_UPGRADE-Nachricht an den Host gesendet,
     der Host übernimmt dieselbe Änderung → kein Überschreiben beim Sync.
   - 3 neue Tests in tests/state.test.js.

3. Tab-Buttons (ui.js):
   - tab-party, tab-dice, tab-system, tab-journal, tab-stats zur DOM-
     Initialisierung hinzugefügt → switchTab() findet Buttons wieder.

4. DM-denkt-Anzeige (ui.js):
   - topbar-thinking-status und topbar-thinking-text zur DOM-Initialisierung
     hinzugefügt → showLoader() kann das Element ein-/ausblenden.

5. Erforderliche Proben scrollbar (index.html):
   - min-h-0 zu action-box-container hinzugefügt, damit overflow-y-auto
     im Flex-Container korrekt greift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update MCP SDK to 1.27.1 with Zod v4 literal extraction patch

SDK 1.27.1 has a bug in Server.setRequestHandler() where the inline
literal extraction checks `v4Def?.value` but Zod v4 stores literals as
`values: [...]` (array). The SDK's own getLiteralValue() handles both
formats correctly but isn't used in setRequestHandler().

Adds scripts/patch-sdk.mjs that runs before build to fix the extraction.
The patch is idempotent and will be a no-op once the SDK fixes the bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: authorization sequence module with modal pattern, DIAN origin tracking, and production fixes

Migrate from Conductor/Master-Detail to Screen+Modal dialog pattern. Add Origin field
to persist DIAN vs Manual source and lock DIAN fields after save. Add cost center filter,
active status column with inactive row styling, DIAN prerequisite pre-check with UX feedback,
and fix SpinEdit int? binding. Production fixes: restore FE type list on DIAN selection switch,
wrap DIAN SOAP call in Task.Run to avoid UI freeze, move post-init code into finally block,
remove unnecessary Refresh() calls, and fix TotalEntries mapping (was using deprecated Count).

commit.message:
feat: schema v12 — universal facets + PJE domain taxonomy

Plan 9 insight: disciplines are namespaces composed at query time, not
directories navigated at storage time. PSH (Polythematic Structured
Subject Headings) model — every entity carries facets, vocabulary grows
through literary warrant.

- universal_facets table: polymorphic entity tagging, any table row
- bootstrap_pje_facets.py: keyword heuristic classifier + --discover
  mode for candidate domain detection
- dual_write.py: facet + facet-query subcommands (14 total)
- sqlite.md rules: universal facets section, query patterns

681 facets bootstrapped across 6 entity types.

commit.message:
fix(sr): disable web search during SR evaluation by default

Search augmentation caused score regression — well-known sources like
foxnews.com drifted from 28% to 48% because search found only 1-2
documented failures (mixed-band cap). Pre-search LLM knowledge was
more accurate. Disabling by default until search quality is calibrated.

Re-enable via UCM Admin → Config → Source Reliability when ready.
